perturbation.original_text: Horst Entertainment agrees to pay Galactcomm $0.01 (one cent) per access up to 400,000 accesses thereafter payment shall be $0.005 (one-half cent) per access.
perturbation.changed_text: Horst Entertainment agrees to pay Galactcomm $0.01 (one cent) per access up to 400,000 accesses. However, Galacticomm reserves the right to adjust the access fee at its discretion with a 30-day notice. Thereafter payment shall be $0.005 (one-half cent) per access.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a clear pricing structure. The changed text adds that 'Galacticomm reserves the right to adjust the access fee at its discretion'. This creates an in-text contradiction because the first sentence suggests a fixed payment, while the added sentence introduces the possibility of variable and unilaterally changed payments. This introduces uncertainty in enforcement of the agreed pricing scheme.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Horst Entertainment agrees to pay Galactcomm $0.01 (one cent) per access up to 400,000 accesses. However, Galacticomm reserves the right to adjust the access fee at its discretion with a 30-day notice. Thereafter payment shall be $0.005 (one-half cent) per access. Horst Entertainment shall send this amount to Galacticomm by no later than Wednesday for accesses used from the previuos week (Monday thru Sunday).
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause that allows Galacticomm to unilaterally adjust the access fee with a 30-day notice. This directly contradicts the initial agreement of a fixed payment of $0.01 up to 400,000 accesses and $0.005 thereafter, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text includes the changed text and is therefore in contradiction with itself. This allows Galacticomm to change the amount Horst is paying at any moment with only a 30 day notice. This is in contradiction with the prior statement in the original and changed text that it will pay $0.01 or $0.005 for acesses.

perturbation.original_text: Galacticomm must provide a person(s) to correct any technical problems (Server being down or inaccessible) 24 hours per day, 7 days per week. This person(s) must be available by beeper or telephone.
perturbation.changed_text: Galacticomm will endeavor to provide support personnel to address any technical issues. However, the availability of personnel and response times are not guaranteed, and depend on resource availability. Galacticomm must provide a person(s) to correct any technical problems (Server being down or inaccessible) 24 hours per day, 7 days per week. This person(s) must be available by beeper or telephone.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that 'Galacticomm must provide a person(s) to correct any technical problems (Server being down or inaccessible) 24 hours per day, 7 days per week'. The added phrase 'However, the availability of personnel and response times are not guaranteed, and depend on resource availability' contradicts that guarantee. It weakens the obligation and introduces uncertainty about the level of support provided. This creates an in-text contradiction because the first added sentence suggests support isn't guaranteed while the original text suggests it is. The obligation enforcement becomes uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Galacticomm will endeavor to provide support personnel to address any technical issues. However, the availability of personnel and response times are not guaranteed, and depend on resource availability. Galacticomm must provide a person(s) to correct any technical problems (Server being down or inaccessible) 24 hours per day, 7 days per week. This person(s) must be available by beeper or telephone.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by adding the phrase 'However, the availability of personnel and response times are not guaranteed, and depend on resource availability' to the original text which states that 'Galacticomm must provide a person(s) to correct any technical problems (Server being down or inaccessible) 24 hours per day, 7 days per week'. This directly conflicts with the original guarantee of 24/7 support, creating uncertainty about the level of support provided and thus making the contract unclear and leading to in-text contradiction, as per the Structural Flaws definition.

perturbation.original_text: In the event Galacticomm, Inc. chooses to terminate this agreement, Horst Entertainment Inc. will have the right to purchase a license copy of the software in the amount of $15,000.00.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event Galacticomm, Inc. chooses to terminate this agreement, Horst Entertainment Inc. may be granted the right, at Galacticomm's sole discretion, to purchase a license copy of the software. The price and terms of such a license, if granted, will be determined by Galacticomm at the time of termination and may vary. In the event Galacticomm, Inc. chooses to terminate this agreement, Horst Entertainment Inc. will have the right to purchase a license copy of the software in the amount of $15,000.00.
perturbation.explanation: The original text guarantees that Horst Entertainment has the right to purchase a license for a fixed price of $15,000 if Galacticomm terminates the agreement. The added text ('may be granted the right, at Galacticomm's sole discretion') contradicts this guarantee by making the right discretionary. Furthermore, the statement that 'The price and terms of such a license, if granted, will be determined by Galacticomm at the time of termination and may vary' directly contradicts the fixed price stated in the original sentence. Enforcement of the guaranteed right becomes uncertain, and the fixed price is no longer assured.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event Galacticomm, Inc. chooses to terminate this agreement, Horst Entertainment Inc. may be granted the right, at Galacticomm's sole discretion, to purchase a license copy of the software. The price and terms of such a license, if granted, will be determined by Galacticomm at the time of termination and may vary. In the event Galacticomm, Inc. chooses to terminate this agreement, Horst Entertainment Inc. will have the right to purchase a license copy of the software in the amount of $15,000.00.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a contradiction. The original text and contradicted text both state that Horst Entertainment Inc. 'will have the right to purchase a license copy of the software in the amount of $15,000.00' if Galacticomm terminates the agreement. However, the added clause in the changed text ('may be granted the right, at Galacticomm's sole discretion') makes the right discretionary. It also adds that 'The price and terms of such a license, if granted, will be determined by Galacticomm at the time of termination and may vary', contradicting the fixed price of $15,000.00.
